data.description:
## Context

The three example skills (/rebar-brainstorm, /rebar-debug, /rebar-implement) each carry their own inline TDD prose. rebar-debug (Steps 4-5) and rebar-implement (Phase 4) are strong on RED->GREEN integrity (RED-first, held-out oracle, mutation check, refactoring litmus, no test-weakening) but under-specified on choosing the RIGHT test: no existing-coverage inventory before authoring, no test-tier selection rule, no precondition/collateral-invariant oracle guidance, no fault-realism rules, no proof the test gates CI. rebar-brainstorm treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs. An external assessment report proposed improvements; this epic adopts the findings judged valid, scaled to the skills' 'scale ceremony to the bug' ethos, as a single shared reference file all three skills load — plus a minimal, guarded addition to the code-review 'tests' criterion for the two meaningful gaps it does not cover (non-gating tests; unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only race sync). Plan-review E5 is deliberately left untouched: it already covers change-detector, proxy-validation (the tier rule at plan level), and missing failure paths, with tuned anti-over-fire guards.

## Success Criteria

- One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.
- The skills' held-out discipline is preserved (no weakening of the held-out oracle model).
- The code-review tests criterion gains only the two uncovered antipatterns, each with explicit false-positive guards, with no posture/routing change.
- CI gates (lint, prompt-index drift) stay green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both child tasks closed with their changes Verified +1 in Gerrit.
- [ ] No change to plan-review E5 or any criteria routing/threshold.
data.title: Shared TDD test-design standard for the example skills + code-review tests-criterion gap fix
